title: Internationally Educated RN Theory
catalogDescription: This course is designed for internationally educated registered nurses (IERNs) to gain a comprehensive understanding of the current concepts, standards, and expectations of nursing practice in the U.S. healthcare system. The course covers essential topics, including the nursing process, pharmacology, and professional nursing roles, with a focus on legal and ethical responsibilities under the Washington State Nurse Practice Act and the Uniform Disciplinary Act. Participants will explore the physical, biological, and social sciences necessary for nursing practice and apply these concepts to diverse patient populations across the lifespan. The course also addresses key areas of registered nursing practice, including medical-surgical, mental health, family, pediatric, community, and geriatric nursing.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Application and Approval Recommended: ENGL& 101 - English Composition I

title: Internationally Educated RN Lab
catalogDescription: This course provides internationally educated registered nurses (IERNs) with intensive hands-on practice to acquire and refine essential nursing skills necessary for clinical practice in the U.S. healthcare system. The course focuses on competency-based learning in key areas such as medical-surgical, mental health, obstetrical, family, pediatric, and geriatric nursing. Through hands-on skills practice and simulated patient care scenarios, participants will enhance their ability to apply advanced clinical skills, critical thinking, and decision-making in a controlled environment. The lab-based approach emphasizes technical proficiency, patient safety, and adherence to Washington State's nursing standards. Participants will also strengthen their communication, teamwork, and patient care management skills in preparation for real-world clinical practice. This is a pass/no credit course.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Application and Approval Recommended: ENGL& 101 - English Composition I

title: Internationally Educated RN Clinical
catalogDescription: This course provides internationally educated registered nurses (IERNs) with hands-on practice in applying advanced nursing knowledge and skills in the clinical environment. The course focuses on developing clinical competencies in medical-surgical, mental health, obstetrical, pediatric, family, and geriatric nursing. Through clinical experiences, participants will hone their clinical decision-making, problem-solving, and critical thinking abilities. Emphasis is placed on patient safety, professional ethics, and collaboration within interdisciplinary healthcare teams. Clinical experiences and simulations will reflect care across the lifespan, preparing participants to provide culturally informed, safe, and effective care in diverse healthcare settings.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Application and Approval Recommended: ENGL& 101 - English Composition I
